title: Weather & Seasons
description: Learn Czech weather expressions (Je hezky/ošklivo/zima/teplo/horko), weather verbs (Prší/Sněží/Svítí slunce), the four seasons with their prepositions, and how to ask about the weather.

exercises.prompt: What does 'Je hezky' mean in English?

data.explanation: 'Je hezky' = It's nice / the weather is nice. 'Je' = it is, 'hezky' = nicely/beautifully. This is the most common positive weather expression.
'Prší' = it's raining. 'Sněží' = it's snowing, 'Svítí slunce' = the sun is shining, 'Fouká vítr' = the wind is blowing.

data.grammar_note: 'Je zima' = it's cold (literally 'it is cold/winter'). 'Dnes' = today. Weather uses 'Je' (it is) + the weather word. 'zima' can also mean 'winter' — context tells you which meaning is intended.

exercises.prompt: Complete the weather expression: 'The sun is shining.'

data.explanation: 'Svítí slunce' = the sun is shining. 'Svítí' = it shines (3rd person singular of 'svítit'). This is a verb-first weather expression — unlike 'Je...' patterns.

exercises.prompt: Translate to English: 'Prší. Vezmi si deštník!'

accepted_answers: It is raining. Take an umbrella!
data.grammar_note: 'Prší' = it's raining (3rd person singular). 'Vezmi si' = take (imperative of vzít si). 'deštník' = umbrella (from 'déšť' = rain).

exercises.prompt: Which of these is the Czech word for 'summer'?

data.explanation: 'léto' = summer (neuter). 'jaro' = spring, 'podzim' = autumn, 'zima' = winter. The four seasons are: jaro, léto, podzim, zima.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the correct preposition: 'in spring'

data.explanation: 'Na jaře' = in spring. Jaro uses 'na' + locative (na jaře), NOT 'v'. Compare: 'v létě' (in summer) uses 'v', but 'na podzim' (in autumn) and 'na jaře' both use 'na'.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the correct preposition: 'in winter'

data.explanation: 'V zimě' = in winter. Zima uses 'v' + locative (v zimě). This is the same pattern as 'v létě' (in summer). The four patterns are: na jaře, v létě, na podzim, v zimě.

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'In autumn the leaves fall' in Czech?

data.question_cz: Jak řeknete 'In autumn the leaves fall' česky?
data.question_en: How do you say 'In autumn the leaves fall' in Czech?
data.explanation: 'Na podzim padá listí.' = In autumn the leaves fall. Podzim uses 'na' + locative (na podzim), not 'v'. 'padá listí' = leaves fall (listí = foliage/leaves, neuter collective noun).

exercises.prompt: Complete the weather conversation. What does person B answer?

lines.speaker: person_a
lines.text: To je dobře! A co zítra?

data.explanation: 'Jaké je dnes počasí?' = 'What's the weather like today?' Standard response uses 'Je' + weather word. 'Je hezky' = it's nice. 'Svítí slunce' = the sun is shining. 'Bude pršet' = it will rain (future). 'Bude ošklivo' = it will be bad weather.
blank_answers: Je hezky. Svítí slunce a je teplo.

exercises.answer_key: Je hezky. Svítí slunce a je teplo.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear (weather description).

data.note: 'Je hezky a svítí slunce.' = 'It's nice and the sun is shining.' 'hezky' = nicely/beautifully (weather context), 'svítí' = shines, 'slunce' = sun (neuter).
